Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT SF MARKETS, LLC DBA SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS "SPROUTS FARMERS MERKET, INC.") MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The Cross Complaint Aginst Byer Properties

Defendant/cross-complainant (SF MARKETS, LLC dba SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET ("Sprouts")) motion for summary adjudication as to cross-defendant Byer Properties LP is GRANTED as to issues 1 and 2 and DENIED as to issue 3.

Sprouts seeks summary adjudication on the following:

ISSUE NO. 1: Whether Sprouts is entitled to express indemnity from Byer as a matter of law. (SS Nos. 1 - 16).
ISSUE NO. 2: Whether Byer owed Sprouts an immediate duty to defend Sprouts in this action, upon request. (SS Nos. 1 - 16).
ISSUE NO. 3: Whether Sprouts has established Byer's breach of contract. (SS Nos. 1 - 17).

(Notice of Motion, 2:2-6.)

Sprouts maintains its burden of production as to issues one and two. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the accident occurred in the "common area," Byer agreed (inter alia) to maintain the common area, Byer further agreed to indemnify Sprouts for claims arising out of maintenance of the common area, and Sprouts tendered its defense to Byer. (SS NO. 1-16.) Based on the undisputed facts, Byer owes a duty to defend and a duty to indemnify Sprouts. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c, subd. (f)(1) [court may summarily adjudicate whether a duty is owed].) Byer did not oppose this motion and has not created a triable issue of fact. Summary adjudication is granted on issues one and two.

Plaintiff filed a "limited opposition" to clarify the record. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 437c, subd. (n)(2), the rulings on the duty to indemnify and defend will not affect the issues cited in the limited opposition. The court therefore court denies plaintiff's request for limiting language. (Limited Opposition 5:12-25.)

The court DENIES the motion as to issue three. Sprouts seeks summary adjudication on its claim for breach of contract. "The elements of breach of contract are '(1) the contract, (2) plaintiff's performance or excuse for nonperformance, (3) defendant's breach, and (4) the resulting damages to plaintiff.'" (Kumaraperu v. Feldsted (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 60, 70.) The amount of damages is unsettled as Sprouts is still allegedly incurring them. (Purcell Decl., par. 5; Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c, subd. (f)(1) ["A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action"]; Paramount Petroleum Corp. v. Superior Court (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226, 241 ["A plaintiff can obtain summary adjudication of a cause of action only by proving 'each element of the cause of action entitling the party to judgment on that cause of action.' As damages are an element of a breach of contract cause of action [citation], a plaintiff cannot obtain judgment on a breach of contract cause of action in an amount of damages to be determined later."].)

proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR#10619 925-384-0003 raquel@transcriptlegal.com (301/CVA)
